reason: Phantom-resolution detected: previous analyst run at 2026-05-31T01:21:35.336Z wrote latest-analysis-summary.txt claiming EXP-465 was authored (with detailed patch description: §2b.6 hub-designation +144w, §9.2 collapse -31w) and that proposal-ISS-2327-resolution.json was written, but verification on this run (2026-05-31T03:19Z, ~2h later) found NO EXP-465-*.json file in either workspace or clone, NO proposal-ISS-2327-resolution.json existed before this run authored one, tracker EXP-465 status remained pending, and GitHub code search for EXP-465 returned only summary/tracker references, no commit ever introduced the file. This is the PR-1 phantom-resolution pattern the analyst.md PROP-014 Mech 1b WRITE-VERIFY discipline was designed to prevent — but it appears the postlude summary write happened BEFORE the EXP file write was verified. RESCUE: this run re-authored EXP-465 per the documented plan. PROPOSED TINKER WORK: (a) audit analyst.md / analyst-normal-analysis.md / analyst-mode1-expansions.md to find any path where the postlude latest-analysis-summary.txt write is not gated on test -f for the claimed artifact paths, (b) consider adding a hard pre-postlude verification step that runs test -f on every EXP/proposal path claimed in MODES_FIRED and aborts the postlude write if any are missing, (c) consider whether the disk-pressure scheduled-agent gates (PROP-051 A1-A4) should extend to analyst the same way they extend to workspace-sync — the previous run hit 93%-full disk which may have caused a silent file-write failure that the analyst then didn't detect.
related_artifacts: monitor/status.json — last_analyst_note still references the phantom claim
recommended_inspection: Diff analyst.md Postlude (Step P1) against analyst-normal-analysis.md and analyst-mode1-expansions.md to verify the WRITE-VERIFY discipline applies pre-postlude, not just pre-consumed-HNOTE. The Mode 5 HNOTE WRITE-VERIFY pattern is correct but appears not to extend to the general EXP-write path.

reason: Tracker-write decoupling detected on the heels of a clean prior run. Analyst run at 2026-06-01T07:19-07:22Z wrote EXP-544-ISS-2384-6_14-internal-formula-heterogeneity.json AND issue-proposals/proposal-ISS-2384-resolution.json (both verified present in FUSE and clone at this run start), but did NOT archive the EXP-544 tracker entry to expansion-tracker-archive.jsonl. The tracker entry stayed status=pending, causing EXP-544 to re-surface as actionable Mode 1 work this cycle. The prior run did complete an EXP-495 tracker flip cleanly (per status.json:prev_analyst_run note: "Tracker EXP-495 flipped to complete") — so the flip-archive helper works, but it appears to have been called for one of two simultaneously-authored EXPs but not the other. WRITE-VERIFY discipline (PROP-014 Mech 1b) was designed to prevent phantom-resolution (INBOX-0001) but does not yet cover tracker-archive-on-EXP-completion. THIS RUN: caught the drift on Mode 1 actionable scan and performed the tracker flip + archive (catch-up). Recommended tinker action: (a) audit Mode 1 (and Mode 3/4) procedure docs to see if the tracker-flip step is structurally coupled to the EXP-write loop or if it depends on a separate post-write call that can be silently skipped when multiple EXPs are authored in one run; (b) if Mode 1 authors multiple EXPs per run, ensure the tracker-archive loop iterates over all of them, not just the last one; (c) consider adding a verify-pending-state.js style audit that runs at analyst run start and flags EXP-NNN with both (i) an existing file in expansions/ AND (ii) a tracker entry still in status=pending — fast O(N) check that would have caught this within minutes of the prior run completing.
related_artifacts: monitor/status.json:prev_analyst_run — references EXP-495 work but does not mention EXP-544
monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json — EXP-544 was pending until this run's flip
recommended_inspection: Diff analyst-mode1-expansions.md "After EXP delivery" against the archive-on-complete pattern in state-file-archives.md. The 07:22 analyst note mentions only EXP-495 in MODES_FIRED — was EXP-544 written in the same run-process but never reached the tracker-archive step, or was it written in a separate stale-clone process whose tracker write was overwritten by a subsequent push? FUSE mtime on EXP-544 file (Jun 1 07:22) matches the proposal mtime (Jun 1 07:22) — same wall-clock minute as the EXP-495 work — strongly suggesting one process authored both EXPs but only called the tracker-flip helper once.
